description: Wire real Federation (Matrix bridge) and make QuantMeet SFU use LiveKit. Replace the in-memory Matrix bridge-bot and room-mapper with real matrix-js-sdk integration. Replace the fake SFU service (random ICE candidates) with LiveKit-backed implementation.
steps: 1. Replace packages/federation/src/matrix/room-mapper.ts - Instead of an in-memory Map, use a proper persistence layer. Create a RoomMappingStore interface with an InMemoryRoomMappingStore (for tests) and a PrismaRoomMappingStore (for production using the existing database package). The store must persist quant-conversation-id <-> matrix-room-id mappings.

2. Replace packages/federation/src/matrix/bridge-bot.ts - Remove the @simulated annotation. Implement a real MatrixBridgeBot that uses the matrix-bot-sdk package. The bot must: connect to a Matrix homeserver (url from env MATRIX_HOMESERVER_URL), authenticate (MATRIX_BOT_TOKEN), listen for messages in bridged rooms, forward messages bidirectionally. Keep the same public interface (onQuantMessage, onMatrixMessage, getForwardedMessages) but wire them to real SDK calls. When MATRIX_HOMESERVER_URL is not set, fall back to the existing in-memory simulation for dev/test.

4. Refactor apps/quantmeet/backend/services/sfu.service.ts - The current implementation generates random ICE candidates and tracks state in-memory. Replace it with a LiveKitSFUService that delegates to the already-existing LiveKitGateway (apps/quantmeet/backend/services/livekit-gateway.service.ts). createTransport should create a LiveKit room, connectTransport should generate participant tokens, produce/consume should use LiveKit's track management.
5. Update apps/quantmeet to properly route SFU calls through LiveKit when LIVEKIT_API_KEY is set, falling back to the current in-memory simulation when not configured.
6. Add tests for the new Matrix bridge (mock matrix-bot-sdk) and LiveKit SFU wrapper (mock livekit-server-sdk).
7. Verify: pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=@quant/federation --filter=@quant/quantmeet && pnpm turbo test --filter=@quant/federation --filter=@quant/quantmeet
acceptance_criteria: Matrix bridge-bot connects to real homeserver when env vars are set
Room mapper persists mappings (not just in-memory Map)
SFU service delegates to LiveKit when configured
Graceful fallback to in-memory mode when external services unavailable
All tests pass including new ones
findings: Used matrix-bot-sdk (0.7.1) successfully. The vi.fn() mock in Vitest 4 requires using a function keyword constructor pattern (not arrow functions) for class mocks. LiveKit integration is seamless since livekit-gateway.service.ts was already real. The SFU service now marks rooms as pending synchronously before the async createRoom call to prevent duplicate room creation. All 347 tests pass (177 federation + 170 quantmeet).
